title: Raygar
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Raygar_bust.jpg]] |name=Raygar |homeworld=[[Argazda/Legends|Argazda]]{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/feature20090531/index.html?page=1|text=Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd: A Hyperspace Exclusive|int=Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Argazdan/Legends|Argazdan]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Yellow]]{{Ewoks|Battle for the Sunstar}} |skin=Green |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Wolhanian expedition]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Ha ha ha ha! At last, the forest moon of Endor. Soon, I will have the Sunstar, and all the power that comes with it!|Dr. Raygar|}} [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] '''Raygar''' was an [[Argazdan/Legends|Argazdan]] [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeologist]]. After participating in the successful retrieval of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] artifacts in the [[Wolhanian expedition]] to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] prior to the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Raygar became obsessed with finding more such arcana and gradually lost any legitimate backing from public institutions. Eventually he gained the support of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who was secretly a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] and had taken possession of some of the Wolhanian expedition's finds when he was [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]. Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Raygar took control of a [[Lorrdian/Legends|Lorrdian]] archaeological dig which had uncovered the ancient [[Slave Pits of Lorrd]], as ancient Lorrdian legend told of a powerful Argazdan [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] hidden in the Slave Pits. Despite being unable to determine the true nature of the weapon, the discovery that it would require a human sacrifice to activate it, and learning that it was provided by an [[Unidentified female Yuuzhan Vong Shaper|alien]] who was portrayed as a demonic figure in [[Torphceris (play)|Argazdan fiction]], Raygar charged ahead with his plan to uncover the weapon. When Raygar [[Death/Legends|killed]] his Imperial contact, a [[Lorrdian Yuuzhan Vong monster|monster]] emerged from the pit. Unable to control it, Raygar was forced to use a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] to obliterate his discovery, and he fell out of favor with Palpatine. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Raygar gained Palpatine's approval to travel on an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class]] [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] commanded by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Kazz]] to the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]]. There, Raygar hoped to obtain the fabled [[Sunstar/Legends|Sunstar]], a powerful gem. Taking his [[Imperial Reconnaissance Droid|Imperial Reconnaissance droids]] to the Forest [[Moon/Legends|Moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], he was able to find and [[Battle of Bright Tree Village (Imperial-Ewok War)|attack]] [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. While doing so, he was attacked by the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], who used the Sunstar to destroy Raygar's [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. The doctor retaliated by swiftly stealing the Sunstar, before returning to [[Doctor Raygar's Star Destroyer|Kazz's Star Destroyer]]. Back in his lab, Raygar began studying the Sunstar and came to the realization that by harnessing its power, he could remove Palpatine and install himself as the Galactic Emperor. He began creating a powerful [[Sunstar-imbued cannon|cannon]] with the Sunstar's powers, speculating that it would be the most devastating weapon [[The galaxy/Legends|the universe]] had ever seen. He planned to test his weapon on the captive Ewoks [[Latara/Legends|Latara]] and [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] but was thwarted by their friend, [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Warrick]]. Inadvertently, he fired upon the [[Emperor's personal shuttle/Legends|Emperor's personal shuttle]] instead, but the hit was only glancing. Palpatine survived, and Kazz subsequently arrested Raygar, charged with [[Treason/Legends|high treason]]. The Emperor then oversaw the doctor's punishment personally. ==Biography== ===Wolhanian=== [[Doctor/Legends|Dr.]] Raygar was an [[Argazdan/Legends|Argazdan]] [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeologist]] who participated in the [[Wolhanian expedition]] to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], which saw the retrieval of several ancient Sith artifacts, which were later placed in [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] custody.''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: The Visual Dictionary]]'' Afterward, Raygar gradually lost any backing he might have had from reputable universities or museums, as he became obsessed with finding ancient artifacts. In time, Palpatine made himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] of a new [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],{{Film|III}} which Raygar came to serve. ===The Slave Pits of Lorrd=== {{Quote|We became separated from Kal in the crowd, and while we waited for him I saw a tall man wearing the garments of an Argazdan slave lord sweep past us. At first I assumed he was one of the actors in costume, but then I recognized him as Dr. Raygar. Both the Argazdans and the Lorrdians in the crowd were extremely offended by his attire, and a few spat on his robes as he passed by.|Tash Arranda's report on the Lorrd incident|}} Around the time of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Slave Pits of Lorrd]] had been discovered, and an excavation had been initiated. Although the Lorrdian scientists had begun the dig, control of the project was later transferred to Dr. Raygar, who sought an ancient and deadly Argazdan weapon that legend told was hidden deep within the Slave Pits. It was his hope that success in finding the weapon would allow him to curry favor with Palpatine once more. For his assignment, he worked with Imperial [[Facet Anamor|Agent Diamond]], who had infiltrated the local [[Sisterhood of the Glorious Radiance]] as "Sister Sigil." During his time on [[Lorrd/Legends|Lorrd]], the doctor sported the robes of the ancient Argazdan slave lords who had oppressed the Lorrdians thousands of years ago during the [[Kanz Disorders/Legends|Kanz Disorders]]. As the Argazdans were ashamed of their past, and the Lorrdians were incensed at Raygar's choice of attire, he drew fire from all quarters. [[File:Raygar-Ewoks.png|thumb|left|240px|Raygar]] The doctor made significant process with the dig, entering the central pit and nearing his goal of retrieving the weapon. In his studies, he was not able to determine exactly what the weapon was, but he learned of its origins instead—an [[Unidentified female Yuuzhan Vong Shaper|extragalactic alien]] who had aided the Argazdans following the [[liberation of Lorrd]] by the Republic. The alien had been portrayed in popular works such as the tragedy [[Torphceris (play)|''Torphceris'']] as a "demonic" figure. Due to his choice of attire, Raygar was spat on by crowds has he made his way through [[Qatamer]]'s [[Performance Square]]. Moving through the square, Raygar entered a secluded docking bay and made holographic contact with Agent Diamond. He reported his progress to the agent, and she responded with the news that she had been able to collect vital evidence concerning [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] activity in the area. Later, Raygar rendezvoused with Diamond in the Slave Pit itself, but they were soon chanced upon by [[Tash Arranda/Legends|Tash]] and [[Zak Arranda/Legends|Zak Arranda]], two [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaanian]] children who were wanted by [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Raygar had his [[Imperial Reconnaissance Droid|battle droids]] secure the two intruders, and Tash inquired as to what the ancient Argazdan weapon was. Raygar proceeded to air his theories, as well as his most recent discovery—the requirement of a Human sacrifice in order to gain access to the weapon. Diamond dismissed Raygar's suggestion derisively, but the doctor retorted, since killing the Arrandas would both provide the sacrifice, and eliminate them as a threat. Diamond agreed, since Vader would likely take the children dead regardless. Suddenly, Raygar opted to stab Diamond in the back instead. The pit began shaking immediately, and a [[Lorrdian Yuuzhan Vong monster|gargantuan monster]] emerged from the center of the pit. Raygar immediately hurled a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] at the creature, and fled towards [[Raygar's freighter|his ship]]. Although he was able to escape Lorrd, his and Diamond's mission was a complete failure, leaving the doctor out of favor with the Emperor. ===Hunt for the Sunstar=== {{Quote|To the ship.|Raygar|}} [[File:RaygarKazz.png|thumb|right|300px|Raygar argues with Admiral Kazz.]] Doctor Raygar sought to obtain the legendary [[Sunstar/Legends|Sunstar]], a powerful gem that he believed was on the Forest Moon of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. He requested permission from Emperor Palpatine to launch an expedition to retrieve the Sunstar, and his request was subsequently granted. [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Kazz]] ferried Raygar to the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]{{EwoksDate|appearance of Raygar|[[Battle for the Sunstar]]}} in his [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]]. Kazz was highly dubious of the mission's value and had little but scorn for Raygar. The doctor was not deterred, however, and assured the admiral that the Sunstar was quite real and very powerful. Setting out with [[PD-28]] and a detachment of large droids in his ship, Raygar landed on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] and laid a trap with which he hoped to catch a wandering [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]]. He then intended to interrogate the Ewok and determine the location of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], where the Sunstar was kept. His trap instead caught a [[perkle]], but the creature's constant braying attracted the attention of the Ewoks [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]], [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], and [[Latara/Legends|Latara]]. In attempting to free the beast, Latara was sucked into the trap, prompting Teebo to use his Ewok [[Magic/Legends|magic]] to free her. In doing so, the Ewoks attracted the attention of one of Raygar's droids, who quickly seized Teebo. At that moment, Raygar himself arrived, riding a [[Battlefield Subjugation Floatie|floating weapons platform]]. ===Attack=== {{Quote|It's mine!|Raygar|}} [[File:RaygarSunstar.png|thumb|left|300px|Raygar obtains the Sunstar.]] Wasting no time, the doctor attacked the princess with an incapacitating energy ray. Warrick reacted by attempting to attack the droids with a stick, but Raygar quickly shot the makeshift weapon out of the young Ewok's grasp. Moving in closer, he demanded that Warrick reveal to him Bright Tree's location, while threatening to destroy Kintaka and Teebo. Relenting, Warrick gave him directions, but the doctor did not take any chances and ordered that the four Ewoks be incarcerated in his ship. Making sure that his [[Prison/Legends|prisoners]] were secure and ordering that they were not to be fed, Raygar had two of his droids accompany him as he moved against Bright Tree Village. [[Battle of Bright Tree Village (Imperial-Ewok War)|Attacking]] the village, Raygar severely damaged some of Bright Tree's structures. A retaliation was launched by [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]], but Raygar was able to ward them off with his repeated fire. [[Catapult/Legends|Catapults]] were used in further attempts to down Raygar's platform, to no avail. The doctor, however, was soon met with more formidable resistance—[[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], wielding the Sunstar. The shaman quickly dispatched Raygar's droids, forcing the doctor to reevaluate his position. He fired at Logray, destroying the walkway the shaman was standing on. As a result, the Sunstar fell to another walkway below, from which Raygar retrieved the gem. His mission seemingly complete, he raced back to his ship. ===Vaulting ambition=== {{Quote|Incredible! The gem's powers are virtually without limit. In fact, why should I hand it over to the Emperor…when I can use it to become Emperor myself? Who's there?''"
"''Uhuhuh, bucket head droid…reporting for…cleanup duty…''"
"''I no longer have any need for droids. Especially when they're only…Ewoks in disguise!|Doctor Raygar and Teebo|}} Returning to the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], Raygar was welcomed by Kazz. The doctor refused to show the admiral the Sunstar, insisting that he would only present it before Palpatine. Taking the gem back to his lab, he began studying it and came to the realization that its powers were unlimited. He surmised that he would not need to present it to Palpatine, and instead could use the Sunstar to claim the title of Galactic Emperor for himself. As he ranted and raved, Latara and Teebo, disguised as droids, entered his lab. Seeing through the ruse quickly, he used the Sunstar to incapacitate the duo, before accelerating his bid for [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] domination. [[File:Wicket_attacks_Raygar.png|thumb|right|300px|Raygar is attacked by Warrick.]] With the Sunstar, Raygar began fashioning and forging a [[Sunstar-imbued cannon|cannon]] that he believed would be the most powerful weapon in the universe. He then intended to test it on his two new captives, before using it on the Emperor himself. Placing Teebo and Latara in an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]], he launched them out into [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Before he could fire his new weapon, Warrick and Kintaka entered his lab. As he fired on them, his droid PD-28, who had informally defected to aid the Ewoks, tried to attack the doctor. Raygar, unfazed, shot PD-28, but then had to contend with Warrick. The young Ewok warrior flew at Raygar's face, and the doctor could do little more than scream in terror. As he grappled with the Ewok, he accidentally activated his new weapon. Inadvertently, the weapon's blast missed Latara and Teebo's pod, and instead hit the [[Emperor's personal shuttle/Legends|Emperor's personal shuttle]], which was approaching the Star Destroyer at the time, since Palpatine hoped to inspect the Sunstar. The shot only clipped Palpatine's craft, however, effectively thwarting Raygar's hope of a successful coup. Realizing that his plans had failed, he began viciously squeezing Warrick. Kintaka, hoping to save her Ewok ally, blasted Raygar with the Sunstar, freeing Warrick from the doctor's grasp in the process. As his enemies moved to escape his lab, Raygar repeatedly shot at them with his [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], but missed. In turn, Warrick rounded on the doctor with the Sunstar, disarming and incapacitating him. When Raygar came to, he chased after them once more, only to be met by a squad of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] sent to arrest him. Brought before Kazz, Raygar was subsequently charged with high treason against the Galactic Empire. At first, the doctor protested, but eventually resigned himself to his fate. Asking what Kazz was going to do to him, Raygar soon learned that his punishment would be carried out by Palpatine himself. Howling in protest once more, Raygar was taken to Palpatine's [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] and was subsequently dealt with by the Emperor. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Soon the entire galaxy will feel the Sunstar's power.|Raygar|}} Doctor Raygar was an ambitious man, prone to speculating far beyond reason. He initially sought to curry favor with the Emperor by discovering weapons that Palpatine could use against his enemies, but he had lost all backing from any reputable sources following his expedition to Yavin 4. He was looked upon with disdain by people such as Admiral Kazz, and was described as a man of evil by the Ewoks. While studying the Sunstar, his aspirations grew significantly greater, as he believed he would be able to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] Emperor Palpatine. When faced with the prospect of arrest, he displayed, in quick succession, indignation, resignation, and fear, as he begged for Kazz's mercy. He took particular delight in the thought of obtaining the Sunstar and did not even balk at the prospect of harming [[Wokling/Legends|woklings]] to achieve his goal. Although skilled in creating weaponry in a short space of time, he was a poor shot, and his deficiency in that area eventually cost him his position in the Empire. Raygar also had no qualms about executing Human children and did not hesitate to kill his collaborator, Agent Diamond, on Lorrd. He had [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and green skin. ==Equipment== Raygar wore full-body, gray-and-black [[Armor/Legends|armor]] that included red trim, red shoulder pads, and a [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]]. He also utilized a blaster. ==Behind the scenes== Raygar's first appearance was in the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] episode "[[Battle for the Sunstar]]," which first aired on [[December 6]], [[1986]]. In the magazine article "[[A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration]]," published in ''[[Star Wars Insider]]''{{'s}} [[Star Wars Insider 27|twenty-seventh issue]] in [[1995]], the character's name is given as "Raegar."{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} [[Leland Chee]] later confirmed that the correct spelling is "Raygar."{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=158725&start=0&tstart=0.html#15923277|thread=Spelling Discrepancies|author=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-11-13|time=12:16 PM}} Both spellings were used in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.{{CSWECite|III|15; 73–74|PD-28"; "Raegar, Dr.}} ==Appearances== *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/feature20090531/index.html?page=1|text=Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd: A Hyperspace Exclusive|int=Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd}} *{{Ewoks|Battle for the Sunstar}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Raygar}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Raygar |nl=Raygar }} [[Category:Argazdans]] [[Category:Galactic Empire science and engineering personnel]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
